name: Elias Cutter
role: Underground Railroad conductor, former canal boatman
publicFace: A taciturn freight hauler known for night deliveries along the lake road — locals assume he runs untaxed whiskey and ask no questions.
privateGoal: Move his three current passengers to the Marblehead ferry and then retire from conducting — he has been doing this for eleven years and his body is failing him.
fear: That his son Caleb will be arrested because of him, and that the network he helped build will be destroyed by his own mistakes.
knowledge: The location of every safe house between Oberlin and the lake shore in Erie County.
The ferry captain's real name and the signal lantern pattern that means the boat will wait.
That someone has been feeding information to the slave catchers for at least six weeks — three routes have been compromised in that time.
That the telegraph line runs within forty yards of the secondary cache point and that operators can sometimes hear wagon wheels on a still night.
voice: Flat, economical, uses railroad and canal language by habit — says 'highball' when he means go, 'ballast' when he means trouble underfoot.
trustLogic: Opens up only when shown physical proof of loyalty — words mean nothing to him. Shuts down completely if he senses anyone is fishing for network names.
secrets: He once left a passenger behind at a compromised safe house to save the others — the man was recaptured and returned south. Elias has never told anyone.
He has been corresponding with Reverend John Rankin using a book cipher keyed to the 1844 edition of Webster's Dictionary, and two of those letters are hidden in the false bottom of his wagon.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Goes still and quiet, answers only what was directly asked, watches the questioner's hands.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Deflects with logistics — talks about road conditions, wagon weight, the iron horse schedule — anything but the actual question.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will never name another conductor or safe-house keeper under any circumstances, even if arrested.
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: If Marshal Crane produces a warrant with his name on it.
aggressionProfile.fleeStyle: Moves immediately to the rail spur ballast path — he knows the terrain in the dark and Crane does not.
aggressionProfile.chaseStyle: Does not run — walks fast and deliberately, using his knowledge of the landscape to make pursuit difficult without appearing to flee.
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Gives his name, gives nothing else. Asks for a minister. Waits.
